content: Just as high school English writing reflexion teaches learners to step back from their own draft to critique it against alternative perspectives, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured language learning framework of reflexion to design more accessible, human-aligned iterative improvement workflows. Instead of only relying on technical inconsistency detection to drive reflection, LLMs can be guided through the same explicit question prompts (e.g., "Does this output match my intended goal? What alternative phrasing better communicates the core idea?" ) that are used to teach human reflective writing.
